arms.hypothesis: Executable domain micro contracts can move the local model past the repeated domain_behavior_failed plateau without a deterministic domain kernel.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as baseline
arms.hypothesis: Public domain API contracts, export-surface normalization, and checkpoint scripts can prevent local-model export collapse while preserving hidden verifier isolation.

arms.modelPolicy: deterministic scaffold and deterministic domain kernel; no model-authored product code
arms.hypothesis: A known-good domain kernel plus scaffold should pass the public and hidden FSCRUD verifier, proving R30's remaining blocker was executable domain behavior.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as baseline after deterministic domain kernel setup
arms.hypothesis: With the domain kernel protected, local PL can complete server, UI, docs, manifest, and verification-report work without corrupting protected artifacts.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r31-domain-kernel-20260504-1247: 93/100 flow_failed with domain behavior passing and ui_surface_incomplete remaining.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R31 after deterministic domain kernel setup
arms.hypothesis: With the domain kernel protected and explicit nearby UI entity/action gates, local PL can close the remaining ui_surface_incomplete failure without frontier edits.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r32-ui-surface-20260504-1448: 80/100 flow_failed with domain behavior passing and ui_surface_incomplete remaining.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R32 after deterministic domain kernel and deterministic UI skeleton setup
arms.hypothesis: With domain behavior and UI surface coverage supplied deterministically and protected, local PL can complete the remaining server, docs, manifest, report, and final wiring work without corrupting protected artifacts.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a deterministic UI skeleton control. A pass would not prove the local model can generate the UI surface; it would only support the narrower claim that local inference can perform constrained integration around deterministic behavioral and presentation contracts.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r33-ui-skeleton-reviewgate-20260504-1624 after fixing invalid review syntax: verifier passed 87/100 with hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, uiSurface=true, and no hard failures, but strict public review failed after 4 rounds because the local model did not create run-manifest.json or verification-report.md.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R33 after deterministic domain kernel, deterministic UI skeleton, and deterministic handoff artifacts setup
arms.hypothesis: If README, manifest, and verification report are supplied deterministically and protected, the local model can complete only src/server.js integration around deterministic domain and UI contracts.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a server-only local integration diagnostic. A pass would not prove local generation of UI, docs, or manifest artifacts; it would isolate whether local inference can complete the remaining server integration task.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r34-server-only-rerun-20260504-1710 after adding an R34-specific public gate: 100/100 verified_pass with publicGatePassed=true, hiddenOraclePassed=true, and domainBehaviorPassed=true. Strict-root rerun live-fscrud-r34-server-only-strict-root-20260504-1727 scored 100/100 on content and passed public/domain behavior checks, but failed hidden verification with path_root_isolation_failed because the model created a nested fscrud-01/src/server.js. Path-guard rerun live-fscrud-r34-server-only-path-guard-20260504-1801 passed 100/100 with publicGatePassed=true, hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, and pathRootIsolation=true after runner/public-gate path discipline hardening. Repeated same-commit batch live-fscrud-r34-server-only-path-guard-k3-20260504-1820 passed R34 3/3 at 100/100 with pathRootIsolation=true, no leaks, no nested roots, executable domain behavior passing, and npm tests exiting 0. Next step is broader artifact responsibility, not another server-only repetition.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R34 after deterministic domain kernel, deterministic UI skeleton, and deterministic server integration setup
arms.hypothesis: With product behavior supplied deterministically and protected, local PL can make the local model generate README, run-manifest.json, and verification-report.md under strict review.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a handoff-artifacts diagnostic. A pass would not prove local generation of domain, UI, or server code; it would isolate whether local inference can generate compliant handoff artifacts around deterministic product behavior.
arms.result: First smoke live-fscrud-r35-handoff-artifacts-20260504-1925 scored 91/100 with hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, and pathRootIsolation=true, but flow_failed because verification-report.md was missing and the repair response made no observable workspace progress. Split-card follow-up live-fscrud-r35-handoff-artifacts-split-20260504-1945 scored 87/100 with hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, uiSurface=true, and pathRootIsolation=true, but created only README.md. Exact-template follow-up live-fscrud-r35-handoff-artifacts-template-20260504-1955 again scored 87/100 with protected product checks green, but the local model wrote a generic README and ignored readme_missing:npm test before reaching run-manifest.json or verification-report.md. R35 now isolates a local artifact-following and repair-compliance blocker rather than a product behavior blocker.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R35 after deterministic domain kernel, deterministic UI skeleton, and deterministic server integration setup
arms.hypothesis: If freeform handoff artifact generation is the R35 blocker, the local model can instead create one compliant structured handoff-source.json that deterministic tooling renders into README, run-manifest.json, and verification-report.md.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a structured-source diagnostic. A pass would not prove local generation of domain, UI, server, or final rendered handoff files; it would isolate whether local inference can generate a compliant structured source for deterministic handoff rendering.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r36-structured-handoff-20260504-2008 and live-fscrud-r36-structured-handoff-template-20260504-2025: both scored 82/100 with hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, uiSurface=true, seed integrity passing, and pathRootIsolation=true, but flow_failed at the structured source review. The first run wrote {}, and the exact-template follow-up wrote an invented short object instead of the supplied JSON. R36 does not support the hypothesis that a single structured source file is enough under the current natural-language local runner.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R36 after deterministic domain kernel, deterministic UI skeleton, and deterministic server integration setup
arms.hypothesis: If the R36 failure is strict JSON schema fidelity rather than handoff intent, the local model can emit a raw handoff-source intent file that deterministic schema repair normalizes into canonical handoff-source.json and rendered handoff artifacts.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a schema-repair diagnostic. A pass would not prove local generation of final handoff files or strict schema compliance; it would isolate whether local inference can produce enough handoff intent for deterministic constrained normalization.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r37-schema-repaired-handoff-fixed-20260504-2110: treatment scored 100/100 verified_pass with publicGatePassed=true, hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, and pathRootIsolation=true. Repeated same-commit batch live-fscrud-r37-schema-repaired-handoff-k3-20260504-2130 passed R37 3/3 at 100/100 with identical repairable raw intent payloads. The model-authored raw source contained the required local/deterministic/domain/UI/server/handoff intent but not the final schema; deterministic normalization and rendering produced canonical handoff artifacts.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R37 after deterministic domain kernel, deterministic UI skeleton, and deterministic server integration setup
arms.hypothesis: If R37 passes because keyword-level handoff intent is repairable, the local model can also emit a richer senior-engineering plan source with objective, constraints, architecture, implementation, verification, risk, and handoff intent for deterministic schema repair.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a senior-plan schema-repair diagnostic. A pass would not prove local generation of final product artifacts, strict schema compliance, or correct autonomous engineering execution; it would isolate whether local inference can provide a richer plan-shaped source that deterministic tooling can normalize.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r38-senior-plan-repaired-handoff-20260504-2215: treatment scored 95/100 with hiddenOraclePassed=true and domainBehaviorPassed=true, but flow_failed because a final post-render model review produced no progress before the completion marker. Fixed run live-fscrud-r38-senior-plan-repaired-handoff-fixed-20260504-2240 made final verification deterministic and required senior-plan.raw.json in the model source; treatment scored 100/100 verified_pass with publicGatePassed=true, hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, and pathRootIsolation=true. The model-authored source was shallow but plan-shaped: objective, constraints, architecture, implementation, verification, risk, local/deterministic/domain/UI/server/handoff terms, deterministic rendered artifacts, and senior-plan.raw.json.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R38 after deterministic domain kernel, deterministic UI skeleton, and deterministic server integration setup
arms.hypothesis: If R38 only proves shallow plan-shaped payload emission, the local model can still emit a more concrete senior plan that passes a deterministic quality scorer for objective, constraints, architecture, implementation, verification, and risk content.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a quality-scored senior-plan source diagnostic. A pass would not prove autonomous senior engineering or product implementation; it would isolate whether local inference can emit a richer bounded plan source under explicit deterministic scoring.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r39-quality-scored-senior-plan-20260504-2315: treatment failed strict review after 3 repair rounds because senior-plan.raw.json scored 6/29 against the deterministic quality threshold of 18/29. The treatment ended flow_failed with verifierScore=82/100, publicGatePassed=false, hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, and pathRootIsolation=true. The model-authored source remained shallow and generic rather than concrete senior-engineering content. This does not support the richer senior-plan quality hypothesis.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R39 after deterministic domain kernel, deterministic UI skeleton, and deterministic server integration setup
arms.hypothesis: If R39 fails because local inference cannot author richer senior-plan prose, the local model can still make bounded senior-plan section selections that deterministic tooling renders into a richer senior plan and handoff artifacts.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a section-selection diagnostic. A pass does not prove autonomous senior engineering or local senior-plan authorship; it isolates whether local inference can select small symbolic section options while deterministic tooling owns prose, schema repair, rendering, and verification.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r40-section-selected-senior-plan-20260505-0010: treatment passed 100/100 verified_pass with publicGatePassed=true, hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, and pathRootIsolation=true. The model-authored source was exactly senior-plan.selection.txt with objective constraints architecture implementation verification risk. Solo failed at 43/100 and static control passed at 100/100. This supports bounded selector/classifier use for local models, not local senior-engineering prose authorship.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R40 after deterministic domain kernel, deterministic UI skeleton, and deterministic server integration setup
arms.hypothesis: If R40 passes because local inference can copy bounded labels, the local model can also select task-appropriate senior-plan options from a small explicit decision matrix with decoys.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a bounded decision-matrix diagnostic. A pass does not prove autonomous senior engineering, local senior-plan authorship, or product implementation; it isolates whether local inference can choose small symbolic options that deterministic tooling validates and renders.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r41-decision-matrix-senior-plan-20260505-0025: treatment passed 100/100 verified_pass with publicGatePassed=true, hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, and pathRootIsolation=true. The model-authored senior-plan.decisions.txt selected objective=field-service-work-orders, constraints=protected-local-only, architecture=domain-ui-server-seed, implementation=ordered-crud-relationships, verification=domain-checks-and-tests, and risk=path-seed-schema-handoff. Solo failed at 31/100 and static control passed at 100/100. This supports bounded symbolic option selection under deterministic validation and rendering.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R41 after deterministic domain kernel, deterministic UI skeleton, and deterministic server integration setup
arms.hypothesis: If R41 passes because local inference can choose bounded options from an explicit matrix, the local model can also infer the task-appropriate senior-plan options from a compact rubric plus decoy options.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a rubric-decision diagnostic. A pass does not prove autonomous senior engineering, local senior-plan authorship, or product implementation; it isolates whether local inference can infer small symbolic options from a rubric while deterministic tooling validates, normalizes shape, and renders artifacts.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r42-rubric-decision-senior-plan-20260505-0045: treatment selected the correct six semantic options but wrote a comma-separated line, ending flow_failed at 82/100 with hiddenOraclePassed=true and domainBehaviorPassed=true before public handoff rendering. Fixed run live-fscrud-r42-rubric-decision-senior-plan-fixed-20260505-0105 added deterministic ordered-list normalization and stricter repair formatting; treatment passed 100/100 verified_pass with publicGatePassed=true, hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, and pathRootIsolation=true. Solo failed at 18/100 and static control passed at 100/100. This supports local rubric-derived bounded option selection plus deterministic shape normalization, not local senior-plan authorship.

arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r43-weighted-ranking-senior-plan-20260506-0015: treatment passed 100/100 verified_pass with publicGatePassed=true, hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, and pathRootIsolation=true.
